subject: Databases
title: Table-Level Locks
description: A short lesson to help you apply Table-Level Locks.

hook.text: ALTER TABLE adds a column. The entire table is locked. No reads, no writes. Everyone waits.

buildup.text: Table locks block everything on the table. They're used for DDL operations and some maintenance tasks.

discovery.text: LOCK TABLE IN EXCLUSIVE MODE explicitly locks a table. Useful for batch operations that must be alone.

twist.text: In Postgres, most ALTER TABLE operations need an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock — the most restrictive kind.

climax.text: Lock duration matters: a 2-second table lock during peak traffic can queue hundreds of waiting connections.

punchline.text: Table locks: nuclear option. Use briefly and during low traffic only.

quiz.question: Why are table-level locks dangerous during peak traffic?
options: They block all operations on the table, causing queued connections and cascading slowdowns
They permanently prevent future queries on the table
Table locks only exist in very old databases
